title: Cosmia quadrivalvis
taxonomy: Cosmia quadrivalvis is classified as a species within the genus Cosmia, which belongs to the family Noctuidae, commonly known as the owlet moths or ear moths. This classification is based on the moth's physical characteristics and its genetic makeup. The family Noctuidae is further grouped into several subfamilies, with Cosmia quadrivalvis being a member of the subfamily Noctuinae. Noctuinae is a subfamily of large moths with distinct characteristics, such as long antennae and stout bodies. This classification helps scientists to understand the relationships and evolutionary history of Cosmia quadrivalvis and other moths within its genus and family.

intro: Cosmia quadrivalvis, also known as the Four-Valved Broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, typically those in the Brassicaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially aiding in the relief of symptoms associated with various health conditions such as arthritis and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Cosmia quadrivalvis include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cosmia quadrivalvis is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it accessible in various forms for traditional medicine and as a potential source of natural remedies.
